scripts.test:pr: BRANCH=test/wisp-$(date +%s) && git checkout -b $BRANCH && echo "wisp test $(date)" >> test-trigger.txt && git add test-trigger.txt && git commit -m 'test: trigger wisp doc sync' && git push -u origin $BRANCH && gh pr create --title 'Test: trigger Wisp' --body 'Automated test PR to trigger Wisp doc sync.' --base main && gh pr merge --squash --delete-branch --yes
scripts.test:pr:docs:
BRANCH=test/wisp-docs-$(date +%s) && git checkout -b $BRANCH && printf '// Audit logging configuration
export const WISP_AUDIT_LOG = process.env.WISP_AUDIT_LOG === "true"
export const WISP_AUDIT_LOG_PATH = process.env.WISP_AUDIT_LOG_PATH ?? ".wisp/audit.log"
' > src/_test-feature.ts && git add src/_test-feature.ts && git commit -m 'feat: add audit logging via WISP_AUDIT_LOG and WISP_AUDIT_LOG_PATH' && git push -u origin $BRANCH && gh pr create --title 'feat: add audit logging support' --body 'Adds two new environment variables:

- `WISP_AUDIT_LOG` (boolean, default: `false`) — when set to `true`, Wisp writes a structured JSON audit log of every documentation sync operation
- `WISP_AUDIT_LOG_PATH` (string, default: `.wisp/audit.log`) — path where the audit log file is written

Useful for teams that need an audit trail of which PRs triggered documentation changes and what was updated.' --base main && gh pr merge --squash --delete-branch --yes && git checkout main && git pull && git rm -f src/_test-feature.ts && git commit -m 'chore: remove test fixture' && git push
